issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The default focus outline is removed using `outline: none` on the `:focus-visible` and `:focus` pseudo-classes. While a custom outline is intended, the subsequent rule at line 264 explicitly removes the outline for elements that do not match `:focus-visible`, potentially leaving keyboard users without a visual indicator in certain browsers or interaction modes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 258: `:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid currentColor; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`
Line 264: `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none; }`

issuesFound.title: touch target below minimum size

issuesFound.title: broken visually-hidden utility
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` class uses `position: static`, `width: auto`, and `height: auto`, which fails to visually hide the content. This results in "screen-reader only" text being visible to all users, cluttering the interface and breaking the intended design for sighted users.

issuesFound.title: motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions for the mobile navigation toggle the `is-open` class and body scroll lock but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the navigation menu has been deployed or retracted.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1545: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` Line 1558: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onViewToggle` function changes the layout mode between grid and list and updates the `aria-pressed` attribute using a string comparison, but the initial state of these buttons is not managed. If the buttons are not updated correctly during the toggle, the state remains ambiguous to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2115: `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, 'true'); // and set 'false' on the sibling button`

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the product filter is applied, the visible product cards are updated and the count badge is refreshed, but there is no immediate live region announcement of the results. Users of screen readers will not know how many products matched their filter criteria without manually searching for the badge.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2081: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory));`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` updates the error message text and visibility but does not set the `aria-invalid` attribute on the input field. Screen readers will not programmatically perceive the field as being in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2615: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: When a field passes validation in the `_renderResult` function, the error message is hidden, but the `aria-invalid` attribute is not reset to `false` or removed. The field may continue to be announced as invalid by assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2609: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function identifies the trigger and drawer but does not explicitly set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger. If the HTML does not include this default, the initial state of the menu is undefined for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1605: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions for the mobile navigation drawer toggle the visibility of the menu and apply CSS classes to the body, but they do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the menu state has changed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1545: `if (_open || !_trigger || !_drawer) return; _open = true; _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true');`
       Line 1558: `if (!_open) return; _open = false; _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onViewToggle` function changes the product grid layout between 'grid' and 'list' modes and updates the `aria-pressed` attribute on the buttons, but the initial state of these buttons is not managed during the `init` phase. Additionally, the `_onViewToggle` function relies on a manual loop rather than ensuring the attribute is updated as part of the state change logic.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2185: `btn.setAttribute('aria-pressed', 'true'); qsa('[data-view-mode]').forEach(b => { if (b !== btn) b.setAttribute('aria-pressed', 'false'); });`

issuesFound.title: dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the product filters are applied, the `_updateCountBadge` function updates the visible text of the count badge. While it sets an `aria-label`, it does not write to an ARIA live region, meaning screen reader users are not automatically informed of how many products remain after a filter change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2145: `_countBadge.textContent = String(count); setAttr(_countBadge, 'aria-label', `${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''}`); liveRegion.announce(`${count} products found.`);`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module handles the visual display of validation errors and updates `aria-describedby`, but it fails to set the `aria-invalid="true"` attribute on the input element itself. Assistive technologies will not perceive the field as being in an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3014: `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl); field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); errorEl.textContent = result.firstError;`

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input

issuesFound.title: Navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The DesktopNav component is a <nav> element without an aria-label or aria-labelledby. When multiple navigation landmarks exist, each must have a unique accessible name for screen reader users to distinguish them.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1035: <nav id="main-nav" className="desktop-nav" aria-label="Primary navigation">

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria-busy
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component does not set aria-busy when isLoading is true. This attribute informs assistive technology that the button is currently busy/loading.
issuesFound.fix: Line 590: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>

issuesFound.title: Active navigation link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in DesktopNav lacks aria-current="page". This attribute helps screen reader users identify which navigation item corresponds to the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1035: <a href={item.href} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>

issuesFound.title: Decorative icon missing aria-hidden

issuesFound.title: Required field visual marker not hidden from assistive technology

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on global :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global `:focus-visible` rule removes the outline (`outline: none`) without providing any alternative visible focus style such as a box‑shadow or colored border. Keyboard‑only users lose a clear focus indicator on all interactive elements.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: The rule `:focus:not(:focus-visible)` removes the outline for mouse‑initiated focus without supplying an alternative visual cue, leaving some focus states invisible.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons lose their outline on keyboard focus (`outline: none`) and no alternative focus style is defined, making it hard for keyboard users to see which button is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Links inside feature cards have `outline: none` on focus‑visible with no replacement style, so keyboard users cannot see the focused link.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ toggle buttons remove the outline on focus‑visible and set a negative outline‑offset, providing no visible focus cue for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes the outline on keyboard focus without any alternative visual indicator, hindering navigation for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle buttons remove the outline on focus‑visible and provide no alternative visual focus style, making it difficult for keyboard users to identify the active toggle.

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets `min-height: 20px`, which is below the WCAG minimum target size of 44 × 44 px, making it hard for users with limited motor control to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: Icon‑only buttons define `min-width: 20px` and `min-height: 20px`, both below the recommended 44 px target area, reducing tap accuracy on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs are styled with `width: 20px; height: 20px;` which are below the 44 px minimum target size, making them difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Negative word‑spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: `word-spacing: -0.2em;` creates overlapping words, reducing readability for users with cognitive or visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 224:   word-spacing: normal; /* remove negative spacing */

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative styling
issuesFound.explanation: `a { text-decoration: none; }` removes the default underline from all links without providing another visual cue (e.g., color change, border) to differentiate links from surrounding text, violating SC 1.4.1.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons animate `transform` on `:active` and include `transform` in their transition list, but no reduced‑motion media query disables or slows this animation, causing issues for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: (fix requires changes in external stylesheet/script)

issuesFound.explanation: `.pricing-card` transitions `transform` and moves upward on hover without a reduced‑motion media query to disable or soften the animation for users preferring reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Inline form validation does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator` renders validation results by calling `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl)` when a field is invalid. `srUtils.markInvalid` only adds an `aria-describedby` reference but never sets `aria‑invalid="true"` on the field, so assistive technologies cannot perceive the error state. Likewise, `srUtils.markValid` does not clear `aria‑invalid`. This violates WCAG 2.1 SC 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation component (`mobileNav`) opens and closes the off‑canvas drawer, but it never updates the `aria‑expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`_trigger`). Without this ARIA state, screen‑reader users cannot know whether the navigation is currently expanded or collapsed, violating WCAG 2.1 SC 4.1.2.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation drawer missing aria‑hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The drawer element (`_drawer`) is shown/hidden by adding/removing CSS classes, but its aria‑hidden state is never updated. When the drawer is closed, screen readers may still read its content.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion triggers do not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: In faqAccordion, the open() and close() functions animate the panel but never set aria‑expanded on the trigger button, leaving the accessible state out of sync with the visual state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers do not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module opens and closes panels but never updates the aria‑expanded attribute on the dropdown trigger button, breaking the required ARIA state communication.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period buttons lack aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The period toggle buttons (`_periodBtns`) receive visual active styling but never reflect their pressed state via aria‑pressed, which is required for toggle buttons.

issuesFound.title: Simple billing‑toggle switch missing aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑toggle element (`_toggleEl`) is given role="switch” but never updates aria‑pressed to indicate its on/off state, violating the ARIA switch pattern.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors are not reflected in aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator module adds error messages and CSS classes but never sets aria‑invalid on the invalid field. Likewise, formAnalytics uses markInvalid which only adds aria-describedby without setting aria‑invalid. Assistive technologies cannot detect the error state.

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Subject” input references missing error ID

issuesFound.title: Contact form “Message” textarea references missing error ID and counter ID together, but the error ID does not exist
issuesFound.explanation: `aria-describedby` includes `'contact-message-error'` which is never rendered.

issuesFound.title: `FormGroup` does not forward the supplied `errorId` to the rendered `ErrorMessage`, causing the error element to be omitted
issuesFound.explanation: `<ErrorMessage id={errorId} message={errorMessage} />` is rendered only when `errorId` is truthy, but most usages pass the error message via the `error` prop (which `FormGroup` does not accept). Consequently, error messages are never rendered, breaking the `aria-describedby` link.
issuesFound.fix:
Update `FormGroup` signature to accept an `error` prop (or rename) and render `<ErrorMessage id={errorId} message={error} />`. Then, in each form usage, pass `errorId` and `error` appropriately. 

These issues collectively address missing accessible names, state communication, label associations, error handling, and keyboard operability throughout the component library.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `:focus-visible` rule sets `outline: none` without providing a visible alternative focus style on the same selector. This removes focus indicators for keyboard users, violating SC 2.4.7 Focus Visible (A).

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-image img:hover` selector applies a `transform` and `box-shadow` transition on hover, but there is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` override to disable or slow this motion. This violates SC 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions (AA).

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The `a { text-decoration: none; }` rule removes underlines from all links, and no alternative non-colour visual differentiator (e.g., font-weight, border-bottom, background highlight) is consistently applied. This violates SC 1.4.1 Use of Color (AA), as links are distinguished from surrounding text only by colour.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` selector sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`, which can impair readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision by compressing word spacing. This violates SC 1.4.12 Text Spacing (AA).
issuesFound.fix: Line 180: Remove `word-spacing: -0.2em;` from the `body` selector.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` selector sets `letter-spacing: -0.05em`, which can impair readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision by compressing letter spacing. This violates SC 1.4.12 Text Spacing (AA).
issuesFound.fix: Line 180: Remove `letter-spacing: -0.05em;` from the `body` selector.

issuesFound.title: Font size below 16px for body text
issuesFound.explanation: The `html` selector sets `font-size: 12px`, which results in body text below the 16px (1rem) minimum recommended for readability. This violates best practices for SC 1.4.4 Resize Text (AA) and impacts users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `.sr-only` utility class incorrectly uses `position: static` and `clip: auto`, which does not hide content visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This violates SC 1.3.1 Info and Relationships (A) and SC 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value (A).

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The function `_onPointerDown` (lines 13678–13767) is attached to a non-native interactive element (the carousel root) via `pointerdown` event. This element is made focusable with `tabindex="0"` but lacks a corresponding `keydown` or `keyup` handler for keyboard interaction (Space/Enter). Users who rely on keyboard navigation cannot activate the drag functionality, violating WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard).

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element

fpTitles: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav._onTriggerClick` function (lines 10234–10243) handles clicks on the mobile navigation toggle button (`.nav-toggle`), but there is no corresponding `keydown` or `keyup` handler for keyboard users (Enter/Space). This means keyboard-only users cannot open or close the mobile navigation menu, violating WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard).

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle element (`.nav-toggle`) uses `addEventListener('click', _onTriggerClick)` but does not have a matching `keydown` handler for Enter/Space keys. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard as the element is not operable via keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` function in `mobileNav` toggles the navigation drawer but does not update `aria-expanded` on the trigger button (`_trigger`). This violates WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 10220: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` function in `mobileNav` announces the navigation state via `liveRegion.announce()` but does not update an adjacent ARIA live region for the dynamic content change (navigation menu opened). This violates WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages.
issuesFound.fix: Line 10225: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen);

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `validateField()` function in `inlineValidator` marks fields as invalid by adding an error class but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input element. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 11000: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `validateField()` function in `inlineValidator` clears the error state by removing the error class but does not set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the attribute. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 11010: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init()` function in `mobileNav` does not initialise `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button (`_trigger`) for the closed navigation drawer. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 10210: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: Focus not managed on modal open
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` function in `mobileNav` opens the navigation drawer but does not move focus into the drawer. This violates WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 10220: requestAnimationFrame(() => { const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus(); });

issuesFound.title: Focus not returned on modal close
issuesFound.explanation: The `close()` function in `mobileNav` closes the navigation drawer but does not return focus to the trigger button (`_trigger`). This violates WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 10230: requestAnimationFrame(() => { if (_trigger) _trigger.focus(); });

issuesFound.title: Focus not trapped in modal
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` function in `mobileNav` opens the navigation drawer but does not trap focus within the drawer. This violates WCAG 2.1.2 No Keyboard Trap.

issuesFound.title: setTimeout/setInterval changing visible content without pause/stop control
issuesFound.explanation: The `play()` function in `testimonialsCarousel` uses `setTimeout` to auto-advance slides but does not provide a user-controllable pause/stop mechanism. This violates WCAG 2.2.1 Timing Adjustable.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10350: (fix requires changes in external stylesheet/script)
    Add a pause/play button and ensure the timeout can be cleared by user action.

issuesFound.title: innerHTML / insertAdjacentHTML without adjacent ARIA live region for dynamic announcements
issuesFound.explanation: The `applyFilters()` function in `productFilter` updates the product grid using DOM manipulation (`appendChild`) but does not update an adjacent ARIA live region to announce the filter change. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages.
issuesFound.fix: Line 10520: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(s.activeCategory));

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing role and keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button (`#scroll-top-btn`) is a `<div>` with a click handler but lacks `role="button"`, `tabindex="0"`, and a `keydown` handler for Enter/Space keys. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value and 2.1.1 Keyboard.

issuesFound.title: aria-controls reference broken
issuesFound.explanation: The `register()` function in `dropdownMenu` sets `aria-controls` on the trigger button to reference the panel ID, but the panel ID is not guaranteed to exist in the DOM at registration time. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.

issuesFound.title: aria-activedescendant reference broken
issuesFound.explanation: The `setActiveIndex()` function in `suggestionListRenderer` sets `aria-activedescendant` on the input to reference a suggestion item ID, but the item ID may not exist in the DOM at the time of setting. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.

issuesFound.title: aria-errormessage reference broken

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attribute on personal data input

issuesFound.explanation: The `<html>` element lacks a `lang` attribute. This violates WCAG 3.1.1 Language of Page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1: <html lang="en-GB">

issuesFound.title: aria-live region not present for dynamic content
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` function in `mobileNav` announces the navigation state but does not have an adjacent `aria-live` region in the DOM. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages.

issuesFound.title: Decorative icon missing aria-hidden={true}
issuesFound.explanation: The social media icons in the SiteFooter component are decorative and rendered as empty spans with CSS classes, but they lack aria-hidden="true". This causes screen readers to announce the presence of the icon without providing meaningful information, creating unnecessary noise.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2803: `<span aria-hidden="true" className="social-icon" />`

issuesFound.title: Required-field marker announces bare asterisk to AT — add aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The required-field marker (asterisk) in the FormGroup component is not hidden from assistive technology. Screen readers announce the asterisk as "star," which is not meaningful without context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1032: ` *`

issuesFound.title: Form field missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The SearchInput component's input field lacks an accessible label. While there is a visually hidden label, the input itself does not have an `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby` prop to associate it with the label. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 and 3.3.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1218: `<TextInput id={id} type="search" value={value} onChange={(ev) => onChange(ev.target.value)} placeholder={placeholder} autoComplete="off" aria-label="Search" className="site-search-form__input" />`

issuesFound.title: Form field missing aria-invalid state
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component does not receive an `aria-invalid` prop when the `invalid` prop is true. This violates WCAG 4.1.2, as screen readers cannot announce the error state of the input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1060: `<input id={id} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-invalid={invalid || undefined} {...rest} />`

issuesFound.title: Interactive state not communicated to assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The FilterTabs component uses the `activeFilter` state to track the selected tab, but the tab buttons do not have an `aria-pressed` or `aria-selected` prop to communicate their state to assistive technology. This violates WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1880: `<button type="button" className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} aria-selected={activeFilter === tab.value} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)}>`

issuesFound.title: Active navigation link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The MobileNav component highlights the current page link with a class (`mobile-nav__link--current`), but it does not use `aria-current="page"` to programmatically indicate the current page to assistive technology. This violates WCAG 1.3.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1420: `<a href={item.href} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} onClick={onClose}>`

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria-busy
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component uses the `isLoading` prop to show a spinner and disable the button, but it does not set `aria-busy={isLoading}` to communicate the loading state to assistive technology. This violates WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 950: `<button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>`

issuesFound.title: Spinner icon missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The spinner icon inside the Button component is decorative and should be hidden from assistive technology, but it lacks `aria-hidden="true"`. This causes screen readers to announce the spinner as part of the button's accessible name.

issuesFound.title: Icon-only interactive element missing accessible name

While it has an `srLabel` prop, the Button component's `srLabel` is not applied here because the `children` prop is the icon. This violates WCAG 1.1.1 and 4.1.2.

issuesFound.explanation: The FormGroup component renders an ErrorMessage when there is an error, but it does not automatically link the error message to the input via `aria-describedby`. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 and 3.3.1.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed globally without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without defining a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. This makes keyboard focus invisible for all elements that do not have a specific override, blocking keyboard-only users from tracking focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on buttons without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule explicitly removes the outline without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border. Keyboard users cannot identify which button is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in High Contrast Mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, :focus-visible is set to outline: none. This removes the system-mandated focus indicator for users relying on high contrast settings.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class fails to hide content
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which renders content visible on screen instead of hiding it visually while keeping it for screen readers. This clutters the interface for sighted users.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing reduces space between words, making text significantly harder to read for users with cognitive or visual disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Base font size set below recommended readability threshold
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size: 12px. This makes the base font size (1rem) 12px, which is below the recommended 16px for body text, reducing readability for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Touch target size below minimum for small buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px. This is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum target size of 24px, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate on touch screens.

issuesFound.title: Touch target size below minimum for icon buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height to 20px. This is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum target size of 24px, hindering activation for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Touch target size below minimum for checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input is explicitly sized to 20px by 20px. This is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum target size of 24px, making it difficult to select for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on primary buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-primary class uses #5588bb background with #ffffff text. The contrast ratio is approximately 3.4:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on danger buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-danger class uses #ee9999 background with #ffffff text. The contrast ratio is approximately 1.6:1, which severely fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative in footer navigation
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have text-decoration: none. The hover state only changes color, relying on color alone to distinguish the link state, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative in social links
issuesFound.explanation: Social links have text-decoration: none. The hover state only changes color, relying on color alone to distinguish the link state, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class defines transitions for transform and box-shadow. There is no effective @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable these animations for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer nav links :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot identify focus within the footer navigation.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on social links
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a :focus-visible rule sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot identify focus on social media icons.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Multiple selectors set `outline: none` on `:focus-visible` without providing a visible alternative such as a border or box-shadow. This removes the focus indicator for keyboard users, making it impossible to identify the active element (WCAG 2.4.7). The global `:focus-visible` rule and specific component overrides all fail to define a visible state.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn-icon` class sets `min-width` and `min-height` to 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum target size of 24px (SC 2.5.8). This makes the button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 379: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn`, `.skip-link`, and `.hero-image img` selectors define `transition` or `transform` properties that cause spatial movement, but the `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block is empty or does not override these specific selectors. This violates WCAG 2.3.3 for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The `body` selector sets `word-spacing: -0.2em`, which condenses text and can significantly reduce readability for users with cognitive or visual disabilities (WCAG 1.4.12).
issuesFound.fix: Line 157: body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; word-spacing: 0; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn-primary` class uses `#5588bb` text on a `#ffffff` background (inherited from `--colour-text-on-brand`), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.38:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text (SC 1.4.3).
issuesFound.fix: Line 369: .btn-primary { background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); color: var(--colour-text-on-brand); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global `a` selector sets `text-decoration: none`, removing underlines from all links. While some components add hover states, the default state relies on colour alone to distinguish links from surrounding text, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 (Use of Color).
issuesFound.fix: Line 153: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class makes content visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class is intended to hide content visually while keeping it available to screen readers. However, it uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which renders the content fully visible on the page. This breaks the intended functionality for skip links or screen-reader-only labels.

issuesFound.title: Base focus-visible rule removes outline without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without defining a replacement visible indicator (such as border or box-shadow). This removes the focus ring for all interactive elements that rely on this base rule, making keyboard navigation impossible to track.

issuesFound.title: Button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector explicitly sets outline: none but does not provide a visible alternative like a border or box-shadow. Keyboard users cannot identify which button is focused.

issuesFound.title: Navigation toggle focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see when the mobile menu button is focused.

issuesFound.title: Navigation link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The #nav-menu > li > a selector relies on the base :focus-visible rule which sets outline: none. No specific focus style is defined for these links, making them invisible to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Feature card link focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot identify focus on these links.

issuesFound.title: Filter tab button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot identify which filter is focused.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot identify focus on billing toggle buttons.

issuesFound.title: Table wrapper focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .table-wrapper:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot identify focus on the scrollable table region.

issuesFound.title: FAQ button focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot identify focus on accordion headers.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing can cause words to overlap or become illegible for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 237: word-spacing: 0em;

issuesFound.title: Skip link transition missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses a transform transition (Line 403), but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block (Line 416) is empty. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the slide animation.

issuesFound.title: Hero image transform missing reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img uses a transform transition (Line 723), but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block (Line 734) does not disable the transform. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the 3D rotation effect.

issuesFound.title: Button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector sets min-height: 32px. This is below the recommended 44px target size for touch interaction, potentially causing difficulty for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Navigation toggle touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle selector sets min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px. This is below the recommended 44px target size for touch interaction.

issuesFound.title: Navigation link touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The #nav-menu > li > a selector sets min-height: 28px. This is below the recommended 44px target size for touch interaction.

issuesFound.title: Search button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] selector sets width: 28px and height: 28px. This is below the recommended 44px target size for touch interaction.

issuesFound.title: Filter tab button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button selector sets min-height: 28px. This is below the recommended 44px target size for touch interaction.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button selector sets min-height: 28px. This is below the recommended 44px target size for touch interaction.

issuesFound.title: Social link touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a selector sets min-height: 28px. This is below the recommended 44px target size for touch interaction.

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation links lack underline on hover
issuesFound.explanation: The footer nav links remove text-decoration and rely solely on color change (Line 1321–1323) to indicate hover state. Users with color vision deficiencies may not perceive the link state change.

issuesFound.title: Social links lack underline on hover
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a selector removes text-decoration and relies solely on color change to indicate hover state. Users with color vision deficiencies may not perceive the link state change.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open function sets the internal _open state to true and updates CSS classes, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the navigation menu is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1674: _open = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav close function sets the internal _open state to false and updates CSS classes, but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the navigation menu is now collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1697: _open = false; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init function does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav init function discovers the trigger element but does not initialize its aria-expanded attribute to "false". Assistive technologies may announce an indeterminate state for the menu trigger on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1745: if (!_trigger.hasAttribute(ARIA.EXPANDED)) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu _openInstance function removes the hidden attribute from the panel and sets internal state, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know the dropdown is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1838: inst.open = true; setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu _closeInstance function hides the panel and sets internal state, but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not know the dropdown is closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1853: inst.open = false; setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register function does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu register function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls on the trigger but does not initialize aria-expanded="false". The initial state of the dropdown is not communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1932: if (!trigger.getAttribute('aria-controls')) setAttr(trigger, 'aria-controls', panelId); setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion open function sets item.open to true and shows the panel, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets "false", creating a state mismatch where the trigger never announces "expanded".
issuesFound.fix: Line 2536: item.open = true; setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils markInvalid function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Assistive technologies rely on aria-invalid to announce that a field contains an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1496: addDescribedBy(field, errorEl); setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils markValid function does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils markValid function removes the error description but fails to remove or set aria-invalid="false" on the field. The field may remain in an invalid state for assistive technologies even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1512: field.removeAttribute('aria-describedby'); field.removeAttribute(ARIA.INVALID);

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop _onClick function does not announce action completion
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop _onClick function scrolls the page and moves focus but does not announce the action to screen readers via a live region. Users may be unaware the scroll action completed successfully.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2006: setTimeout(() => { returnEl.focus({ preventScroll: true }); liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page'); }, 300);

issuesFound.title: pricing _updateSavingsMessage function does not announce dynamic content
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing _updateSavingsMessage function updates text content and visibility of the savings message but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the new savings information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2288: _savingsMsg.textContent = (STRINGS.savingsMessage || 'Save {pct}% with {period} billing').replace('{pct}', String(bp.discountPct)).replace('{period}', bp.label.toLowerCase()); liveRegion.announce(_savingsMsg.textContent);

issuesFound.title: productFilter _updateCountBadge function does not announce count change

issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator such as a box-shadow or border-color change. Keyboard users will not be able to see which element has focus.

issuesFound.fix: Line 1100-1106: .comparison-section tbody td[aria-label="Not included"], .comparison-section tbody td[aria-label="Not available"] { color: var(--colour-neutral-500); font-size: var(--font-size-xl); }

issuesFound.title: Root font size below recommended 16px
issuesFound.explanation: The html selector sets font-size: 12px, which is below the recommended 16px base size. This requires users to zoom more to meet text spacing requirements and can affect readability.
issuesFound.fix: Line 152: html { scroll-behavior: smooth; text-size-adjust: 100%; -webkit-text-size-adjust: 100%; font-size: 16px; line-height: var(--line-height-normal); tab-size: 4; -moz-tab-size: 4; height: 100%; }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu triggers do not update their aria-expanded state when panels are shown or hidden, failing to communicate the disclosure state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line in _openInstance: `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`
  Line in _closeInstance: `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly/annual billing period toggle buttons do not set aria-pressed to indicate which period is currently active, leaving screen reader users unaware of the selected state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not setting aria-invalid on validation error
issuesFound.explanation: When a field fails validation, the aria-invalid attribute is not set to "true", so screen readers do not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, String(!result.valid));`

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not clearing aria-invalid on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid or is cleared, the aria-invalid attribute remains set or is not explicitly set to "false", potentially leaving screen readers with stale error state information.
issuesFound.fix: Line: `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" on page load, leaving assistive technologies unaware that the menu is initially collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are not initialized with aria-expanded="false" when registered, failing to indicate their initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter dynamic update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the product count updates, the change is not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware that the grid has been filtered and how many results are shown.
issuesFound.fix: Line: `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly and annual billing periods updates the price visually but does not announce the change to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line: `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer is not announced to screen readers, leaving users unaware that the menu state has changed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line in open: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen);`
  Line in close: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed);`

issuesFound.title: Toast notifications lack pause mechanism for auto-dismiss
issuesFound.explanation: Toast notifications automatically dismiss after a timeout without a mechanism to pause, stop, or extend the timer, violating WCAG 2.2.1 for users who need more time to read the content.
issuesFound.fix: Add mouseenter/mouseleave and focusin/focusout listeners to the toast element to clear and restart the timer:

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus management on open
issuesFound.explanation: Opening the mobile navigation drawer does not move focus to the first focusable element inside the drawer, leaving keyboard users with focus still on the trigger or in the background content.
issuesFound.fix: Line: `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus return on close
issuesFound.explanation: Closing the mobile navigation drawer does not return focus to the trigger button, disorienting keyboard users who expect focus to return to the element that opened the dialog.
issuesFound.fix: Line: `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();`

fpTitles: inlineValidator not clearing aria-invalid on valid input
Toast notifications lack pause mechanism for auto-dismiss
mobileNav missing focus management on open

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation module initializes, it does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button to indicate that the drawer is initially collapsed. Assistive technologies may not recognize the button as controlling an expandable region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1880: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_trigger.addEventListener('keydown', _onTriggerKeydown);`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu open and close instance functions show and hide the panel content but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the current state. This prevents screen readers from announcing whether the dropdown is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2020: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` in _openInstance. Line 2030: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` in _closeInstance.

issuesFound.title: pricing period toggle buttons missing aria-pressed initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The period toggle buttons are not initialized with aria-pressed attributes when the pricing module loads. The buttons function as toggle buttons but lack the necessary state information for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2960: Add `_periodBtns.forEach(btn => { setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, btn.getAttribute('data-period') === _pricingState.period); });` after discovering the buttons.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field as invalid, the function updates aria-describedby to link the error message but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers will not announce the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1620: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing validation errors from a field, the function removes the error description from aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="false" or remove the attribute. The field remains marked as invalid for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1630: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` after updating aria-describedby.

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the product count updates, the change is visible but not announced to screen readers. Users relying on assistive technologies are not informed how many products match their selected filters.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2680: Add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);` after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch billing periods, the price values update visually but the change is not announced to screen readers. Users may be unaware that prices have changed or what the new amounts are.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2900: Add `liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);` after updating the plan cards.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens, the focus trap is created but never activated, allowing keyboard focus to escape the drawer and move behind the overlay to background content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1810: Add `_trap.activate();` after `_lockScroll();` in the open function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close missing focus return
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, focus is not programmatically returned to the trigger button that opened it. Keyboard users are left with focus at the bottom of the DOM or in an unpredictable location.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1850: Add `_trigger.focus();` at the end of the close function before emitting the close event.

issuesFound.title: breadcrumbHelpers missing aria-current implementation
issuesFound.explanation: The code contains a comment indicating the last breadcrumb item should be marked as the current page, but the implementation is missing. The aria-current attribute is never set on the last link or span, failing to identify the current page location.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2280: Add `if (lastLink) lastLink.setAttribute('aria-current', 'page'); else lastItem.querySelector('span')?.setAttribute('aria-current', 'page');` after the comment.

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard progress bar missing aria-valuemin/max
issuesFound.explanation: The progress bar element is updated with aria-valuenow and aria-valuetext, but the code does not ensure aria-valuemin and aria-valuemax are set. Screen readers cannot calculate the percentage completion without these attributes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3980: Add `_progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuemin', '0');` and `_progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuemax', '100');` in the init function after discovering the progress bar.

fpTitles: mobileNav close missing focus return
multiStepWizard progress bar missing aria-valuemin/max

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function missing focus trap activation and aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer opens without activating the focus trap (createFocusTrap) and without setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This violates WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order) and 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) as keyboard users cannot navigate within the drawer and screen readers are not informed of the expanded state.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When closing the mobile navigation, the code deactivates the focus trap but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving screen readers with stale state information.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button is not initialized with aria-expanded="false" on page load, violating WCAG 4.1.2 as the initial state is not communicated to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not set to "true", failing to communicate the expanded state to screen readers (WCAG 4.1.2).

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu closes, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not set to "false", leaving assistive technologies with incorrect state information.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons are initialized with aria-haspopup and aria-controls, but aria-expanded is not initialized to "false" on page load, violating WCAG 4.1.2.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item expands, the trigger button does not receive aria-expanded="true", preventing screen readers from announcing the expanded state (WCAG 4.1.2).

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion close function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item collapses, the trigger button does not receive aria-expanded="false", leaving screen readers with stale expanded state information.

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing aria-pressed on toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) act as two-state toggles but do not update aria-pressed to reflect which button is active, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator markInvalid missing aria-invalid assignment
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the field is not marked with aria-invalid="true", preventing screen readers from identifying the invalid state (WCAG 3.3.1 Error Identification and 4.1.2).

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator markValid missing aria-invalid clearing
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation passes, aria-invalid is not set to "false" or removed, leaving fields incorrectly marked as invalid for screen readers.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init missing aria-expanded on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox but aria-expanded is not initialized to "false" on page load, violating WCAG 4.1.2. The attribute is required for combobox widgets to indicate whether the popup is displayed.

issuesFound.title: newsletterForm email input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter email input field collects personal data (email address) but lacks the autocomplete="email" attribute, violating WCAG 1.3.5 Identify Input Purpose.

issuesFound.title: contactForm full name input missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The contact form full name input (selector '[name="full_name"]') collects personal data but lacks autocomplete="name", violating WCAG 1.3.5 Identify Input Purpose.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation opens or closes, the state change is not announced via an ARIA live region, leaving screen reader users unaware that the UI has changed (WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages).

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown open/close actions update the visual UI but do not announce state changes via an ARIA live region, violating WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ expand/collapse actions do not announce the state change or the content being revealed via an ARIA live region, violating WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages.

issuesFound.title: productFilter results not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the updated product count and results are not announced via an ARIA live region, leaving screen reader users unaware that the content has changed (WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages).

fpTitles: dropdownMenu _closeInstance missing aria-expanded update
newsletterForm email input missing autocomplete attribute
contactForm full name input missing autocomplete attribute
faqAccordion state changes not announced to screen readers

issuesFound.explanation: The button controlling the mobile navigation panel has aria-controls but no aria-expanded attribute to communicate the menu's open/closed state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line: SiteHeader component: <button ... aria-expanded={isOpen} ...>

issuesFound.title: FaqItemComponent accordion trigger missing aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop, but ThemeToggle renders it without one. This results in a button with no accessible name, making it unusable for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: ThemeToggle component: <IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} ...>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The close button is rendered as an IconButton without the required aria-label prop, leaving it without an accessible name for screen reader users.

issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton for the next slide has no aria-label prop, making it inaccessible to screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: Textarea missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When the invalid prop is true, the component does not set aria-invalid="true" on the textarea element, preventing screen readers from announcing the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Textarea component: <textarea ... aria-invalid={invalid ? "true" : undefined} ...>

issuesFound.title: Button missing aria-busy when loading

issuesFound.fix: Line: BillingToggle component: <button id="billing-monthly" ... aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} ...> and <button id="billing-annual" ... aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} ...>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav close IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component used to close the mobile navigation panel is rendered without the required aria-label prop. The button contains only the "✕" character, which is not announced by all assistive technologies, leaving screen reader users unaware of the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~860: `<IconButton aria-label="Close navigation menu" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">✕</IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle button uses the IconButton component without an aria-label prop. It contains only an emoji (☀️/🌙/💻) as its child, which may not be announced consistently by screen readers, preventing users from understanding the button's function.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~878: `<IconButton aria-label={LABELS[theme]} onClick={() => setTheme(NEXT[theme])} className="theme-toggle">{ICONS[theme]}</IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton used for the carousel previous slide control lacks an aria-label prop. It contains only the "‹" character, which may be announced as punctuation or ignored by screen readers, leaving users unaware of the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1312: `<IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">‹</IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton used for the carousel next slide control lacks an aria-label prop. It contains only the "›" character, which may be announced as punctuation or ignored by screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1316: `<IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">›</IconButton>`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav section trigger missing aria-expanded state

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references 'newsletter-email-error' in aria-describedby when invalid, but FormGroup is not passed the errorId prop, so no element with that ID exists in the DOM. This breaks the programmatic association between the input and its error message.

Line ~1420: `<FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>`
  Fix: Line ~1430: `aria-describedby={email.state.touched && email.state.error ? 'newsletter-email-error' : undefined}` (ensure this remains)

issuesFound.title: SearchInput aria-label overrides visible label text
issuesFound.explanation: The search input has an associated label with text "Search Luminary Studio", but the aria-label="Search" attribute overrides this, causing the accessible name to be only "Search" and losing the context that this is a site-wide search.

issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links marked as current (item.current) receive a visual modifier class but lack aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot identify which page is currently active in the navigation.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links in the mobile menu marked as current receive a visual modifier class but lack aria-current="page", preventing screen reader users from identifying the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~920: `<a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose}>`

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle annual button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The annual billing toggle button indicates selection via CSS class but lacks aria-pressed, preventing screen reader users from understanding the toggle state.

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading state
issuesFound.explanation: When the Button component is in a loading state (isLoading=true), it sets aria-disabled but does not set aria-busy="true". Screen reader users are not informed that the button is currently processing an action.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~690: `<button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>`

issuesFound.title: StarRating star symbols not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The visual star symbols (★/☆) are rendered as text content within spans that are not aria-hidden. The parent span has an aria-label describing the rating, but the star characters may still be announced as "black star" or "white star" by some screen readers, creating redundant verbosity.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~650: `{stars.map((filled, i) => ({filled ? '★' : '☆'}))}`

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown chevron not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron indicator (▲/▼) is rendered as text content without aria-hidden. Screen readers may announce "black up-pointing triangle" or similar, which is redundant since the expanded state is conveyed via aria-expanded (once added) and the button label already indicates it opens a menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~808: `{isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'}`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel non-visible slides not hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Carousel slides that are not currently visible lack aria-hidden="true". All slides remain in the accessibility tree, causing screen reader users to encounter content that is not visually displayed and appears out of context.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav dialog missing aria-modal

issuesFound.title: Modal component aria-modal misplaced on overlay
issuesFound.explanation: The Modal component places aria-modal="true" on the overlay div instead of the dialog element itself. The aria-modal attribute should be on the element with role="dialog" to properly indicate to assistive technology that the content outside the dialog is inert.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on small buttons

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on icon buttons

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] rule sets width: 20px and height: 20px, below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum of 24×24 pixels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1448: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Broken screen-reader-only utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto, which does NOT hide content visually. This is the opposite of the intended behavior—content meant to be screen-reader-only will be visible to sighted users, and the class fails its purpose per WCAG 2.4.6 and technique G1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 337-346: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Empty prefers-reduced-motion media query
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty and does not override any transitions or animations. Motion-sensitive transitions on .btn elements (transform, box-shadow) will still animate for users who prefer reduced motion, violating WCAG 2.3.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 345-346: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } .hero-image img { transition: none; } .feature-card { transition: none; } .product-card { transition: none; } .pricing-card { transition: none; } .stat-item { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Empty reduced-motion override for hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for .hero-image img contains empty rules that do not disable the transform transition defined on line 730. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the 3D rotation effect.
issuesFound.fix: Line 765-768: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element has word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces spacing between words and can impair readability for users with cognitive disabilities or reading difficulties, violating WCAG 2.1.1 and readability best practices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 244: Remove word-spacing: -0.2em; from the body rule (keep the rest of the properties).

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed without alternative visual indicator
issuesFound.explanation: The rule a { text-decoration: none; } removes underlines from all links globally. Links in body text are distinguished from surrounding text by colour alone, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color. There is no .prose a rule to restore underlines for body content links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 284: Remove the rule or add: .prose a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Base font size below recommended minimum
issuesFound.explanation: The html element sets font-size: 12px, which is below the recommended 16px (1rem) baseline. This makes all text smaller than optimal for readability, particularly affecting users with low vision or reading difficulties.
issuesFound.fix: Line 239: html { scroll-behavior: smooth; text-size-adjust: 100%; -webkit-text-size-adjust: 100%; font-size: 16px; line-height: var(--line-height-normal); tab-size: 4; -moz-tab-size: 4; height: 100%; }

issuesFound.title: Paragraph line-height too low
issuesFound.explanation: The p element has line-height: 1, which is below the recommended minimum of 1.5 for body text. This reduces readability for users with dyslexia, cognitive disabilities, or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 289: p { line-height: 1.5; text-wrap: pretty; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on stat labels
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label rule sets color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) (white at 30% opacity) on a gradient background. White at 30% opacity on a medium-dark background results in extremely low contrast (approximately 1.4:1), far below the 4.5:1 minimum required by WCAG 1.4.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 995: .stat-label { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.85); line-height: var(--line-height-snug); max-width: 160px; text-align: center; margin: 0; }

issuesFound.explanation: The a rule sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. Links are distinguished from surrounding text by colour alone, which violates WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color. Users with colour vision deficiencies may not be able to identify links in body text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 130: a { text-decoration: underline; } (or add border-bottom for links in body text contexts)

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm rule sets min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target size of 24px. With padding of 4.5px (0.375rem at 12px root), the total touch target is approximately 29px, still below the recommended 44px.
issuesFound.fix: Line 418: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-4); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon rule sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, both below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum of 24px. Icon-only buttons require adequate touch targets for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 424: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-3); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] rule sets width: 20px and height: 20px, below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum of 24px. Checkboxes are critical interactive controls that require adequate touch targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1176: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has transform and transition properties that create motion effects, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block is empty and does not disable or reduce these animations. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience the 3D transform effect, violating WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 447-450: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transform: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }
