issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not move focus into drawer on open
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, focus is not moved into it. Keyboard users must manually tab to reach the navigation, and screen reader users may not realize the drawer has opened.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2619: add `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus({ preventScroll: true });` after `_lockScroll();`

issuesFound.title: productFilter does not announce filter results via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When product filters are applied and the visible count changes, screen reader users are not notified of the results. The _updateCountBadge() function updates the visible count but does not announce it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4758: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(count) || `${count} products shown`);` at the end of the `applyFilters()` function after `_syncPagination(totalMatches);`

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod does not announce billing period change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled between monthly and annual, screen reader users are not notified of the change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 6280: add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? (STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing — save 20%') : (STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly || 'Prices switched to monthly billing'));` after `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', ...);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion does not announce state change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() and close() functions have an announceToSR parameter that is never used. Screen reader users are not notified when FAQ items expand or collapse.
issuesFound.fix: Line 5421: add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqOpened(item.question) || `${item.question} — answer expanded`);` after `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:open', { item });` and Line 5455: add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.faqClosed(item.question) || `${item.question} — answer collapsed`);` after `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:close', { item });`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not announce state change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and close() functions have an announceToSR parameter that is never used. Screen reader users are not notified when the navigation opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2619: add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` after `_lockScroll();` and Line 2651: add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` after `_unlockScroll();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() does not set aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, the code removes CSS classes and unlocks scroll but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not activate focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable for focus trapping but never assigns or activates it. When the navigation drawer opens as a modal overlay, focus is not trapped within it, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2473: `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer); _trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() does not return focus to trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period toggle buttons missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncToggleState` function toggles CSS classes on billing period buttons but does not update `aria-pressed`. Toggle buttons must expose their pressed state to assistive technologies so screen reader users know which option is active.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle switch missing aria-checked attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The toggle switch element (`_toggleEl`) is assigned `role="switch"` in the init function, but `aria-checked` is never set or updated. A switch role requires `aria-checked` to communicate its state to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The `_syncTabAriaStates` function toggles CSS classes on filter tab buttons but does not update `aria-pressed`. Filter buttons that act as toggles must expose their pressed state to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger not initialised with aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init()` function does not set `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button at initialisation. When a disclosure widget is closed on page load, the trigger must have `aria-expanded="false"` so assistive technologies correctly report the initial state.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers not initialised with aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu.register()` function sets `aria-haspopup` on trigger buttons but does not initialise `aria-expanded="false"`. Dropdown triggers must have `aria-expanded="false"` when the dropdown is closed on page load.

issuesFound.title: Theme switcher buttons missing keyboard activation handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `themeSwitcher.init()` function adds a click handler to `[data-theme-toggle]` buttons but does not add a corresponding keydown handler for Enter and Space keys. Interactive elements activated by click must also be keyboard accessible.

issuesFound.title: Product filter count change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_updateCountBadge()` function updates the visible product count text but does not announce the change to screen readers. Dynamic content changes that convey meaningful information must be announced via a live region.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `setPeriod()` function updates prices and UI state when the billing period changes, but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users of assistive technologies need to know when the pricing display has changed.

fpTitles: Theme switcher buttons missing keyboard activation handler

issuesFound.title: MobileNav toggle button missing aria-expanded attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button changes its aria-label based on open/close state, but lacks the aria-expanded attribute. Screen readers need aria-expanded to programmatically convey the expanded/collapsed state of the controlled panel.

issuesFound.title: ThemeToggle IconButton missing required aria-label prop
issuesFound.explanation: The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop (enforced by TypeScript interface), but ThemeToggle does not pass one. The button becomes an icon-only button without an accessible name, making it unusable for screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: Modal has aria-modal on overlay instead of dialog element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay div, but it should be on the element with role="dialog". This violates ARIA specification and may cause screen readers to incorrectly announce the modal's boundaries.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav active link missing aria-current="page"
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link uses a CSS class to indicate active state visually, but lacks aria-current="page" to communicate this programmatically to screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: MobileNav active link missing aria-current="page"
issuesFound.explanation: The current page link in mobile navigation uses a CSS class for visual indication but lacks aria-current="page" for screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: HeroSection missing aria-labelledby for landmark
issuesFound.explanation: The hero section is a landmark region without an accessible name. While it contains a heading, the section lacks aria-labelledby pointing to that heading, making it harder for screen reader users to navigate by regions.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1078-1081:
```tsx
    <section
      className="hero-section"
      aria-labelledby="hero-heading"
    >
```

issuesFound.title: StatsBar section missing aria-labelledby for landmark
issuesFound.explanation: The stats section has a visually hidden heading with id="stats-heading" but the section element lacks aria-labelledby pointing to it. Screen reader users cannot identify this region by its accessible name.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1185-1188: ```tsx

Luminary Studio by the numbers

```

issuesFound.title: StatItem value lacks accessible name for formatted numbers
issuesFound.explanation: Statistics like "4.2M" and "40,000+" are displayed visually but announced as "four point two em" or "forty thousand plus" by screen readers. The data includes valueLabel ("4.2 million", "40,000 plus") but it is not exposed to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Spinner decorative ring missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The spinner component's visual ring element is decorative but lacks aria-hidden="true". Screen readers may announce the empty span element, creating redundant or confusing output alongside the existing accessible label.

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading state
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component communicates loading state through a spinner and visually hidden text, but lacks aria-busy="true" when isLoading. Screen readers may not reliably announce that the button is in a busy/loading state.

issuesFound.title: FormGroup required marker asterisk should be hidden from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The required field asterisk (*) is part of the label text and will be announced as "Full name asterisk" by screen readers. While a visually hidden "(required)" suffix is provided, the asterisk itself should be hidden to avoid redundant announcement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 685-692: ```tsx ```

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs category buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter toggle buttons indicate active state through CSS class only (filter-tab-btn--active) but lack aria-pressed to communicate the selected state to screen reader users.

'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>

issuesFound.explanation: The theme toggle button contains only an emoji icon (☀️, 🌙, or 💻) with no accessible name. The IconButton component requires aria-label but none is passed, leaving screen reader users unable to identify the button's purpose.

issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay div instead of the dialog div with role="dialog". Per ARIA specifications, aria-modal must be on the element with role="dialog" to properly communicate modality to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop and applies error styling but does not set aria-invalid="true" when the field has a validation error. Screen readers cannot announce the invalid state to users.

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop and applies error styling but does not set aria-invalid="true" when the field has a validation error. Screen readers cannot announce the invalid state to users.

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current page link missing aria-current

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Mobile navigation links with item.current set to true receive a visual indicator (mobile-nav__link--current class) but lack aria-current="page" to programmatically indicate the current page to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading state
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component has an isLoading state that shows a spinner and disables the button, but does not set aria-busy="true" to communicate the loading state to screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal attribute placed on wrong element
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal="true" attribute is placed on the overlay container div, but it should be on the element with role="dialog" (the inner dialog div). This prevents screen readers from properly understanding the modal's boundaries.
